name: Market Microstructure Analytics
description:
The Hidden Toll on Every Trade

Every time you buy or sell a financial instrument, you pay a cost that never appears on your brokerage statement. It is not a commission. It is not a fee. It is the spread between the price at which someone is willing to sell to you and the price at which someone is willing to buy from you. That gap, measured in ticks, basis points, or fractions of a percent, is the bid-ask spread. Over a single trade it looks small. Over thousands of trades, across a year, for …

description: The indicator draws two horizontal lines around price — an upper and a lower. name: BoaBias: MTF ALMA SuperTrend & Stats
description:
What it does

ALMA + SuperTrend on 15m, 1H, 4H, 1D, 3D, 1W — one chart. Each TF has its own line and signal. Filled bands show alignment (green = shorter above longer, red = below). Optional bar coloring by ALMA signal on your chosen TF.

How it works

ALMA: Gaussian-weighted MA. Length, sigma, offset. Uses ta.alma() from TradingView ta library.

SuperTrend: Upper = ALMA + factor × stdev, lower = ALMA − factor × stdev. Flips on band cross. Price above = bullish, below = bearish.

MTF: …

name: Fair Value Gap [Hary]
description:
A Fair Value Gap (FVG) Version 6 — also called an imbalance — is a 3-candle price pattern where the market moves so fast in one direction that it leaves an unfilled gap between the wicks of the first and third candles. Price often returns to "fill" these gaps.

How detection works
FVG TypeConditionBullishlow > high — current candle's low is above the 2-bar-ago highBearishhigh < low — current candle's high is below the 2-bar-ago low

name: PVT Trend Channel
description:
The PVT Trend Channel indicator is a trend-following tool based on the Price Volume Trend (PVT). PVT is a cumulative indicator that takes both price movement and trading volume into account — meaning a trend signal is only triggered when price moves with real volume behind it. This makes it significantly more reliable than indicators that rely on price alone.
